truth_boundary: Public-safe Trismegistus source cards. These cards support concept, architecture, and demo-boundary answers. They do not prove full private autonomy, official benchmark scores, live payments, live outreach, or a hosted Hermes provider unless the matching runtime receipt exists.

cards.title: Source Mirror Pattern / Mirror Architecture
cards.claim: Source Mirror Pattern is the public-facing concept spine behind Trismegistus: preserve long-context research continuity, compare baseline and architecture-on routes, separate proof from inference, and use receipts plus next gates instead of unsupported done claims.
cards.evidence: STACK_FLOW.md describes Trismegistus as an AI Expert Partner with Mirror Architecture / SSP, C5B / Golden Mark, SQL + JSON memory, RAG/source tables, mission queues, browser/source tools, Telegram, Mac Mail, Stripe sandbox gates, GitHub/code work, receipts, and separated benchmark lanes. The public paper frames Trismegistus as a partner that preserves continuity, compares routes, improves through receipts, and moves research toward real work.
cards.boundary: This supports concept and product architecture language. It is not a claim that every private worker lane is active on Render.
cards.next_gate: Use a live generated chat turn plus a saved turn receipt before claiming hosted inference, then run lane-specific receipts for benchmark or worker claims.

cards.title: Hosted Render Demo Boundary
cards.claim: The hosted demo is a public review surface: judges can click it, see the app move, ask concept/source questions, and inspect runtime truth without being told the full private stack is live.
cards.evidence: The public package boundary says to show process, code, selected receipts, and package story while avoiding claims of official leaderboard placement, paid revenue, or live autonomous spend/send/action without exact external receipts.
cards.boundary: Render does not automatically include local private memory, browser sessions, OpenClaw/NemoClaw workers, Telegram, Mac Mail, Stripe, or benchmark harness state.
cards.next_gate: Deploy the corrected runtime image, verify /api/runtime, verify /api/chat returns a generated model turn, and keep unsupported lanes gated.

cards.title: Trismegistus Public Stack Features
cards.claim: Trismegistus is framed as conversational first, proof-gated when asked, and organized around Hermes baseline, Mirror Architecture / SSP architecture-on routing, SQL/JSON memory, RAG/source tables, worker tools, and receipt saving.
cards.evidence: STACK_FLOW.md lists six lanes: AI/agent architecture, quantum/circuits/math, structured matter/physical systems, life sciences/medical research, Mirror Architecture/Golden Mark evidence, and relationship/paid-work field operations.
cards.boundary: The public hosted app can expose the shape and source cards. Each lane still needs its own live receipt before stronger claims.
cards.next_gate: Keep chat natural by default; switch to claim/evidence/boundary/next-gate only when the user asks for proof, source, audit, benchmark, or receipt support.

cards.title: Benchmark Lane Separation
cards.claim: Benchmark claims must stay separated by lane: C5B/Golden Mark, SWE-bench, WebArena, and GAIA are not interchangeable.
cards.evidence: The stack flow routes receipts into benchmark lanes after mission/tool output, and the public boundary warns against public leaderboard, WebArena, or GAIA claims before their exact receipts exist.
cards.boundary: A demo answer about benchmark architecture is not an official score.
cards.next_gate: For any benchmark prompt, name the lane, show the receipt if present, and state the missing receipt if absent.

cards.title: Source / Browser / Worker Mission Tools
cards.claim: The Tris worker architecture separates browser/source missions from normal chat: Playwright/CDP handles browser action traces, Firecrawl-style ingestion supports source packs, and receipts become SQL/JSON/RAG memory.
cards.evidence: TRIS_BROWSER_AUTONOMY_STACK_2026-06-21.md describes Playwright primary, CDP host attachment, Firecrawl source-ingestion sidecar, WebArena/BrowserGym staging, saved traces, and field-mission bridges.
cards.boundary: The public Render demo should not claim the private/local browser worker is operating unless the current deployment saved the trace receipt.
cards.next_gate: When asked for live research or source fetch, run the source/browser mission and answer from its saved receipt.

cards.title: Claim / Evidence / Boundary / Next Gate Output
cards.claim: Trismegistus should avoid fake completion by answering with claim, evidence, boundary, and next gate when proof is requested.
cards.evidence: The public package language separates what can be said from what must not be claimed without exact receipts.
cards.boundary: This format is for proof mode. Normal chat should remain conversational.
cards.next_gate: Use the format only when a user asks for proof, sources, audit, benchmark, or a receipt.
